name: Ordinary Least Squares (OLS) Linear Regression
applicable_scenarios: fit a linear relationship between a continuous response y and one or more predictors X
baseline model whenever residuals can reasonably be treated as iid Gaussian
closed-form parameter estimation on small-to-medium tabular data
python_template:
import numpy as np

def ols_fit(X: np.ndarray, y: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray:
    # X shape (n, p). Add an intercept column if desired.
    X1 = np.hstack([np.ones((X.shape[0], 1)), X])
    beta, *_ = np.linalg.lstsq(X1, y, rcond=None)
    return beta

def ols_predict(X: np.ndarray, beta: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray:
    X1 = np.hstack([np.ones((X.shape[0], 1)), X])
    return X1 @ beta
typical_cases: predict housing price from area and bedrooms
fit calibration curve for a sensor
common_pitfalls: multicollinearity makes X^T X near-singular; prefer lstsq over inv
outliers dominate squared loss; inspect residuals before trusting beta
non-linear relationships yield biased fits; consider feature engineering or kernel methods
heteroscedastic residuals break the standard error formulas
